cannibalizationRisks.resolution: Homepage shows today's matches as primary content; /today is the canonical destination. Homepage uses a different title and intent (live hub). No direct cannibalization.

/groups is the hub — its title and H1 cover 'all groups'. Individual group pages own specific group queries ('Group D standings', 'Turkey group', etc.). Parent/child relationship, not cannibalization.
/stats is the hub; /stats/top-scorers owns the scorer-specific intent. Clearly differentiated.

/teams/turkey owns team identity, fixtures, squad. /groups/group-d owns the group-wide table. /teams/turkey/qualification owns the 'can turkey qualify' intent. No overlap — each has a distinct canonical intent.
/today?date= is noindex; /matchdays/2026-06-23 is the canonical permanent page for that date. /schedule is the full-season hub with no specific-date content.
/world-cup-third-place-qualification owns the ranking/methodology intent; /qualified-eliminated-teams owns the broader tracker intent (includes all 3 states). Both are complementary and cross-linked.
